release_notes: V14.44 build 224 restores dependable 1-hour mini-trend recovery for the later parallel runtime. The mini-trend now starts its own lightweight one-hour Nightscout history request 15 seconds after startup, retries independently until useful glucose coverage is available, uses strict lightweight SGV and date parsing, anchors live samples to their actual Nightscout entry timestamps, and no longer counts zero-value gap placeholders as a successful history backfill. Existing alarm acknowledgement, parallel audio and WhatsApp runtime, Web UI heap protection, runtime diagnostics and OTA safety functions are preserved.
